general.default_response: Hello 👋, I couldn't fully understand your question or it may not be related to this topic. Please try asking about the manager responsibilities, legal, HR, or fiscal obligations.

Legal Responsibilities.title: Legal Responsibilities
Legal Responsibilities.content: As the manager (Gérant) of a Tunisian SUARL, you are the primary legal representative of the company. Your main legal responsibilities include: maintaining proper accounting records, ensuring compliance with the company bylaws (objet social), providing reports to the sole partner, signing contracts, and being liable for management faults (fautes de gestion).

Human Resources & Asset Management.title: HR & Asset Management
Human Resources & Asset Management.content: Human resources management involves compliance with the Tunisian Labor Code (employment contracts, CNSS contributions, occupational health). Asset management requires maintaining a register of company property (laptops, phones, vehicles) and protecting the company's assets.

Fiscal & Social Obligations.title: Fiscal & Social Obligations
Fiscal & Social Obligations.content: Fiscal obligations include filing corporate tax (IS), monthly VAT (TVA), withholding tax (RS), and TFP/FOPROLOS payments. Social obligations require quarterly CNSS declarations and payment of social security contributions before the 15th of the month following the quarter.

Monthly/Quarterly/Annual Tasks.title: Monthly/Quarterly/Annual Tasks
Monthly/Quarterly/Annual Tasks.content: Monthly: Collect invoices and submit tax declarations before the 28th. Quarterly: Submit CNSS declarations before the 15th. Annually: Conduct stock and asset inventory, file 'Déclaration Employeur', finalize AGO, and pay Corporate Tax (IS).
